title: Closures

learningObjectives.text: Explain lexical environment using a new example and justify the result.
Explain retained variables using a new example and justify the result.
Explain practical closure patterns using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Closures is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Data Structures and Functional Techniques** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Lexical environment** → **Retained variables** → **Practical closure patterns**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Lexical environment
sections.body: A closure keeps access to the lexical environment in which a function was created, even after the outer call has returned.

sections.title: Retained variables
sections.body: Closures retain bindings rather than frozen copies. Later calls observe the current value of a captured binding.

sections.title: Practical closure patterns
sections.body: Closures support factories, private state, memoization, and event handlers. Use them when remembered context clarifies the API, not to hide arbitrary mutable state.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The returned function retains access to the `attempts` binding.

commonMistakes: Thinking a closure stores a frozen copy instead of retaining access to a binding.
Hiding mutable state without a clear public API.
Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
summary: Closures connects Lexical environment, Retained variables, Practical closure patterns.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
